arts.ttl: Spurs confirmed team vs Brentford - Bissouma on the bench as defender misses out
arts.sum: Here's the Tottenham team that Postecoglou has chosen: Vicario; Porro, Romero, Van de Ven, Udogie; Kulusevski, Bentancur, Maddison; Johnson, Solanke, Son. Subs: Forster, Spence, Dragusin, Gray, Sarr, Bissouma, Bergvall, Werner, Moore. Ange Postecoglou has named the Tottenham team that he believes can beat Brentford in Premier League on Saturday afternoon.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou hits back at critics of Dominic Solanke
arts.sum: "We don't have to rush to make judgement all the time because the alternative is he may have got off to a great start, he is fit, scored in all four games, is flying and then he goes through a patch like all strikers where he doesn't score. What I look at is he has come in, fitted really well and quite logically has picked up an injury that has disrupted the way he wanted to start his career, but he's still got plenty of time for that."

arts.ttl: Report: Tottenham think they have a player who’s about to seriously improve

arts.ttl: We simulated Tottenham vs Brentford to predict score for Premier League clash
arts.sum: We predict that Tottenham could line up in a 4-3-3 formation, with their starting XI as follows: Vicario; Porro, Romero, Van de Ven, Udogie; Sarr, Bentancur, Maddison; Kulusevski, Solanke, Son. After a 1-0 loss to Arsenal last weekend, Tottenham will be looking to avoid a third-consecutive Premier League defeat when they host Brentford later today.

arts.ttl: Striker who Tottenham and Arsenal looked at over summer finally ignites at new club
arts.sum: Ivan Toney is finally off the mark as an Al-Ahli player, putting in a stunning performance on Friday. The England international was heavily linked with a number of top Premier League clubs over the summer but in the end, switched Brentford for the Saudi Pro League. It’s been a slow start to life at his […] The post Striker who Tottenham and Arsenal looked at over summer finally ignites at new club appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Tottenham vs Brentford: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Tottenham just about avoided a crisis in midweek and are next in Premier League action on Saturday as they take on Brentford in N17. Saturday's visitors sit above Tottenham in the table having accumulated six points from their opening four games and gave Manchester City a scare last weekend before falling to a 2-1 defeat.
